alerts.title: List Dependabot alerts for an enterprise

parameters.name: enterprise
parameters.description: The slug version of the enterprise name.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of states. If specified, only alerts with these states will be returned.

Can be: auto_dismissed, dismissed, fixed, open

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of severities. If specified, only alerts with these severities will be returned.

Can be: low, medium, high, critical

parameters.name: ecosystem
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of ecosystems. If specified, only alerts for these ecosystems will be returned.

Can be: composer, go, maven, npm, nuget, pip, pub, rubygems, rust

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of package names. If specified, only alerts for these packages will be returned.

parameters.name: epss_percentage
parameters.description:
CVE Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS) percentage. Can be specified as:

  • An exact number (n)
  • Comparators such as >n, <n, >=n, <=n
  • A range like n..n, where n is a number from 0.0 to 1.0

Filters the list of alerts based on EPSS percentages. If specified, only alerts with the provided EPSS percentages will be returned.

parameters.name: has
parameters.description: Filters the list of alerts based on whether the alert has the given value. If specified, only alerts meeting this criterion will be returned. Multiple has filters can be passed to filter for alerts that have all of the values. Currently, only patch is supported.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The scope of the vulnerable dependency. If specified, only alerts with this scope will be returned.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The property by which to sort the results. created means when the alert was created. updated means when the alert's state last changed.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The direction to sort the results by.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: first
parameters.description: Deprecated. The number of results per page (max 100), starting from the first matching result. This parameter must not be used in combination with last. Instead, use per_page in combination with after to fetch the first page of results.

parameters.name: last
parameters.description: Deprecated. The number of results per page (max 100), starting from the last matching result. This parameter must not be used in combination with first. Instead, use per_page in combination with before to fetch the last page of results.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

alerts.descriptionHTML:
Lists Dependabot alerts for repositories that are owned by the specified enterprise.

The authenticated user must be a member of the enterprise to use this endpoint.

Alerts are only returned for organizations in the enterprise for which you are an organization owner or a security manager. For more information about security managers, see "Managing security managers in your organization."

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo or security_events scope to use this endpoint.

security_advisory.summary: Django allows remote attackers to obtain potentially sensitive information by leveraging data exposure from the confirm_login_allowed() method, as demonstrated by discovering whether a user account is inactive
security_advisory.description: django.contrib.auth.forms.AuthenticationForm in Django 2.0 before 2.0.2, and 1.11.8 and 1.11.9, allows remote attackers to obtain potentially sensitive information by leveraging data exposure from the confirm_login_allowed() method, as demonstrated by discovering whether a user account is inactive.

cwes.name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

example.dismissed_comment: This alert is accurate but we use a sanitizer.

security_advisory.summary: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File in ansible
security_advisory.description: A flaw was found in ansible. Credentials, such as secrets, are being disclosed in console log by default and not protected by no_log feature when using those modules. An attacker can take advantage of this information to steal those credentials. The highest threat from this vulnerability is to data confidentiality.

cwes.name: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File

items.description: A Dependabot alert.

state.description: The state of the Dependabot alert.

name.description: The unique package name within its ecosystem.

name.description: The unique package name within its ecosystem.

url.description: The REST API URL of the alert resource.

html_url.description: The GitHub URL of the alert resource.

created_at.description: The time that the alert was created in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

updated_at.description: The time that the alert was last updated in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

dismissed_at.description: The time that the alert was dismissed in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

dismissed_reason.description: The reason that the alert was dismissed.

dismissed_comment.description: An optional comment associated with the alert's dismissal.

fixed_at.description: The time that the alert was no longer detected and was considered fixed in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

auto_dismissed_at.description: The time that the alert was auto-dismissed in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

dismissal_request.title: Dependabot alert dismissal request
dismissal_request.description: Information about an active dismissal request for this Dependabot alert.

id.description: The unique identifier of the dismissal request.

status.description: The current status of the dismissal request.

requester.description: The user who requested the dismissal.

id.description: The unique identifier of the user.

login.description: The login name of the user.

created_at.description: The date and time when the dismissal request was created.

url.description: The API URL to get more information about this dismissal request.

id.description: A unique identifier of the repository.

node_id.description: The GraphQL identifier of the repository.

name.description: The name of the repository.

full_name.description: The full, globally unique, name of the repository.

html_url.description: The URL to view the repository on GitHub.com.

fork.description: Whether the repository is a fork.

url.description: The URL to get more information about the repository from the GitHub API.

archive_url.description: A template for the API URL to download the repository as an archive.

assignees_url.description: A template for the API URL to list the available assignees for issues in the repository.

blobs_url.description: A template for the API URL to create or retrieve a raw Git blob in the repository.

branches_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about branches in the repository.

collaborators_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about collaborators of the repository.

comments_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about comments on the repository.

commits_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about commits on the repository.

compare_url.description: A template for the API URL to compare two commits or refs.

contents_url.description: A template for the API URL to get the contents of the repository.

contributors_url.description: A template for the API URL to list the contributors to the repository.

deployments_url.description: The API URL to list the deployments of the repository.

downloads_url.description: The API URL to list the downloads on the repository.

events_url.description: The API URL to list the events of the repository.

forks_url.description: The API URL to list the forks of the repository.

git_commits_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git commits of the repository.

git_refs_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git refs of the repository.

git_tags_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git tags of the repository.

issue_comment_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issue comments on the repository.

issue_events_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issue events on the repository.

issues_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about issues on the repository.

keys_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about deploy keys on the repository.

labels_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about labels of the repository.

languages_url.description: The API URL to get information about the languages of the repository.

merges_url.description: The API URL to merge branches in the repository.

milestones_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about milestones of the repository.

notifications_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about notifications on the repository.

pulls_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about pull requests on the repository.

releases_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about releases on the repository.

stargazers_url.description: The API URL to list the stargazers on the repository.

statuses_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about statuses of a commit.

subscribers_url.description: The API URL to list the subscribers on the repository.

subscription_url.description: The API URL to subscribe to notifications for this repository.

tags_url.description: The API URL to get information about tags on the repository.

teams_url.description: The API URL to list the teams on the repository.

trees_url.description: A template for the API URL to create or retrieve a raw Git tree of the repository.

hooks_url.description: The API URL to list the hooks on the repository.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

alerts.title: List Dependabot alerts for an organization

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of states. If specified, only alerts with these states will be returned.

Can be: auto_dismissed, dismissed, fixed, open

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of severities. If specified, only alerts with these severities will be returned.

Can be: low, medium, high, critical

parameters.name: ecosystem
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of ecosystems. If specified, only alerts for these ecosystems will be returned.

Can be: composer, go, maven, npm, nuget, pip, pub, rubygems, rust

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of package names. If specified, only alerts for these packages will be returned.

parameters.name: epss_percentage
parameters.description:
CVE Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS) percentage. Can be specified as:

  • An exact number (n)
  • Comparators such as >n, <n, >=n, <=n
  • A range like n..n, where n is a number from 0.0 to 1.0

Filters the list of alerts based on EPSS percentages. If specified, only alerts with the provided EPSS percentages will be returned.

parameters.name: artifact_registry_url
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of artifact registry URLs. If specified, only alerts for repositories with storage records matching these URLs will be returned.

parameters.name: artifact_registry
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of Artifact Registry name strings. If specified, only alerts for repositories with storage records matching these registries will be returned.

Can be: jfrog-artifactory

parameters.name: has
parameters.description: Filters the list of alerts based on whether the alert has the given value. If specified, only alerts meeting this criterion will be returned. Multiple has filters can be passed to filter for alerts that have all of the values.

parameters.name: runtime_risk
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of runtime risk strings. If specified, only alerts for repositories with deployment records matching these risks will be returned.

Can be: critical-resource, internet-exposed, sensitive-data, lateral-movement

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The scope of the vulnerable dependency. If specified, only alerts with this scope will be returned.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The property by which to sort the results. created means when the alert was created. updated means when the alert's state last changed.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The direction to sort the results by.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: first
parameters.description: Deprecated. The number of results per page (max 100), starting from the first matching result. This parameter must not be used in combination with last. Instead, use per_page in combination with after to fetch the first page of results.

parameters.name: last
parameters.description: Deprecated. The number of results per page (max 100), starting from the last matching result. This parameter must not be used in combination with first. Instead, use per_page in combination with before to fetch the last page of results.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

alerts.descriptionHTML:
Lists Dependabot alerts for an organization.

The authenticated user must be an owner or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint. If this endpoint is only used with public repositories, the token can use the public_repo scope instead.

security_advisory.summary: Django allows remote attackers to obtain potentially sensitive information by leveraging data exposure from the confirm_login_allowed() method, as demonstrated by discovering whether a user account is inactive

cwes.name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

An attacker can take advantage of this information to steal those credentials. The highest threat from this vulnerability is to data confidentiality.

alerts.title: List Dependabot alerts for a repository

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of states. If specified, only alerts with these states will be returned.

Can be: auto_dismissed, dismissed, fixed, open

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of severities. If specified, only alerts with these severities will be returned.

Can be: low, medium, high, critical

parameters.name: ecosystem
parameters.description:
A comma-separated list of ecosystems. If specified, only alerts for these ecosystems will be returned.

Can be: composer, go, maven, npm, nuget, pip, pub, rubygems, rust

parameters.name: package
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of package names. If specified, only alerts for these packages will be returned.

parameters.name: manifest
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of full manifest paths. If specified, only alerts for these manifests will be returned.

parameters.name: epss_percentage
parameters.description:
CVE Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS) percentage. Can be specified as:

  • An exact number (n)
  • Comparators such as >n, <n, >=n, <=n
  • A range like n..n, where n is a number from 0.0 to 1.0

Filters the list of alerts based on EPSS percentages. If specified, only alerts with the provided EPSS percentages will be returned.

parameters.name: has
parameters.description: Filters the list of alerts based on whether the alert has the given value. If specified, only alerts meeting this criterion will be returned. Multiple has filters can be passed to filter for alerts that have all of the values. Currently, only patch is supported.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The scope of the vulnerable dependency. If specified, only alerts with this scope will be returned.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The property by which to sort the results. created means when the alert was created. updated means when the alert's state last changed.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The direction to sort the results by.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Deprecated. Page number of the results to fetch. Use cursor-based pagination with before or after instead.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: first
parameters.description: Deprecated. The number of results per page (max 100), starting from the first matching result. This parameter must not be used in combination with last. Instead, use per_page in combination with after to fetch the first page of results.

parameters.name: last
parameters.description: Deprecated. The number of results per page (max 100), starting from the last matching result. This parameter must not be used in combination with first. Instead, use per_page in combination with before to fetch the last page of results.

alerts.title: Get a Dependabot alert

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: alert_number
parameters.description: The number that identifies a Dependabot alert in its repository. You can find this at the end of the URL for a Dependabot alert within GitHub, or in number fields in the response from the GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/dependabot/alerts operation.

alerts.descriptionHTML: OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint. If this endpoint is only used with public repositories, the token can use the public_repo scope instead.

security_advisory.summary: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File in ansible
security_advisory.description: A flaw was found in ansible. Credentials, such as secrets, are being disclosed in console log by default and not protected by no_log feature when using those modules. An attacker can take advantage of this information to steal those credentials. The highest threat from this vulnerability is to data confidentiality.

cwes.name: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File

alerts.title: Update a Dependabot alert

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: alert_number
parameters.description: The number that identifies a Dependabot alert in its repository. You can find this at the end of the URL for a Dependabot alert within GitHub, or in number fields in the response from the GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/dependabot/alerts operation.

bodyParameters.name: state
bodyParameters.description: The state of the Dependabot alert. A dismissed_reason must be provided when setting the state to dismissed.

bodyParameters.name: dismissed_reason
bodyParameters.description: Required when state is dismissed. A reason for dismissing the alert.

bodyParameters.name: dismissed_comment
bodyParameters.description: An optional comment associated with dismissing the alert.

bodyParameters.name: assignees
bodyParameters.description: Usernames to assign to this Dependabot Alert. Pass one or more user logins to replace the set of assignees on this alert. Send an empty array ([]) to clear all assignees from the alert.

alerts.descriptionHTML:
The authenticated user must have access to security alerts for the repository to use this endpoint. For more information, see "Granting access to security alerts."

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the security_events scope to use this endpoint. If this endpoint is only used with public repositories, the token can use the public_repo scope instead.

bodyParameters.dismissed_comment: This alert is accurate but we use a sanitizer.

security_advisory.summary: Django allows remote attackers to obtain potentially sensitive information by leveraging data exposure from the confirm_login_allowed() method, as demonstrated by discovering whether a user account is inactive
security_advisory.description: django.contrib.auth.forms.AuthenticationForm in Django 2.0 before 2.0.2, and 1.11.8 and 1.11.9, allows remote attackers to obtain potentially sensitive information by leveraging data exposure from the confirm_login_allowed() method, as demonstrated by discovering whether a user account is inactive.

cwes.name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

example.dismissed_comment: This alert is accurate but we use a sanitizer.

secrets.title: List organization secrets

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Lists all secrets available in an organization without revealing their encrypted values.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

items.title: Dependabot Secret for an Organization
items.description: Secrets for GitHub Dependabot for an organization.

name.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.title: Get an organization public key

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Gets your public key, which you need to encrypt secrets. You need to encrypt a secret before you can create or update secrets.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

schema.title: DependabotPublicKey
schema.description: The public key used for setting Dependabot Secrets.

secrets.title: Get an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Gets a single organization secret without revealing its encrypted value.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

schema.title: Dependabot Secret for an Organization
schema.description: Secrets for GitHub Dependabot for an organization.

name.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.title: Create or update an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

bodyParameters.name: encrypted_value
bodyParameters.description: Value for your secret, encrypted with LibSodium using the public key retrieved from the Get an organization public key endpoint.

bodyParameters.name: key_id
bodyParameters.description: ID of the key you used to encrypt the secret.

bodyParameters.name: visibility
bodyParameters.description: Which type of organization repositories have access to the organization secret. selected means only the repositories specified by selected_repository_ids can access the secret.

bodyParameters.name: selected_repository_ids
bodyParameters.description: An array of repository ids that can access the organization secret. You can only provide a list of repository ids when the visibility is set to selected. You can manage the list of selected repositories using the List selected repositories for an organization secret, Set selected repositories for an organization secret, and Remove selected repository from an organization secret endpoints.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Creates or updates an organization secret with an encrypted value. Encrypt your secret using LibSodium. For more information, see "Encrypting secrets for the REST API."

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

secrets.title: Delete an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Deletes a secret in an organization using the secret name.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

secrets.title: List selected repositories for an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Lists all repositories that have been selected when the visibility for repository access to a secret is set to selected.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

items.title: Minimal Repository
items.description: Minimal Repository

size.description: The size of the repository, in kilobytes. Size is calculated hourly. When a repository is initially created, the size is 0.

pull_request_creation_policy.description: The policy controlling who can create pull requests: all or collaborators_only.

advanced_security.description:
Enable or disable GitHub Advanced Security for the repository.

For standalone Code Scanning or Secret Protection products, this parameter cannot be used.

dependabot_security_updates.description: Enable or disable Dependabot security updates for the repository.

status.description: The enablement status of Dependabot security updates for the repository.

secrets.title: Set selected repositories for an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

bodyParameters.name: selected_repository_ids
bodyParameters.description: An array of repository ids that can access the organization secret. You can only provide a list of repository ids when the visibility is set to selected. You can add and remove individual repositories using the Set selected repositories for an organization secret and Remove selected repository from an organization secret endpoints.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Replaces all repositories for an organization secret when the visibility for repository access is set to selected. The visibility is set when you Create or update an organization secret.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

secrets.title: Add selected repository to an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Adds a repository to an organization secret when the visibility for repository access is set to selected. The visibility is set when you Create or update an organization secret.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

response.description: No Content when repository was added to the selected list

statusCodes.description: No Content when repository was added to the selected list
Conflict when visibility type is not set to selected

secrets.title: Remove selected repository from an organization secret

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Removes a repository from an organization secret when the visibility for repository access is set to selected. The visibility is set when you Create or update an organization secret.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

response.description: Response when repository was removed from the selected list

statusCodes.description: Response when repository was removed from the selected list
Conflict when visibility type not set to selected

secrets.title: List repository secrets

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Lists all secrets available in a repository without revealing their encrypted values.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

items.title: Dependabot Secret
items.description: Set secrets for Dependabot.

name.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.title: Get a repository public key

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Gets your public key, which you need to encrypt secrets. You need to encrypt a secret before you can create or update secrets. Anyone with read access to the repository can use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint if the repository is private.

schema.title: DependabotPublicKey
schema.description: The public key used for setting Dependabot Secrets.

secrets.title: Get a repository secret

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Gets a single repository secret without revealing its encrypted value.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

name.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.title: Create or update a repository secret

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

bodyParameters.name: encrypted_value
bodyParameters.description: Value for your secret, encrypted with LibSodium using the public key retrieved from the Get a repository public key endpoint.

bodyParameters.name: key_id
bodyParameters.description: ID of the key you used to encrypt the secret.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Creates or updates a repository secret with an encrypted value. Encrypt your secret using LibSodium. For more information, see "Encrypting secrets for the REST API."

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

secrets.title: Delete a repository secret

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.description: The name of the secret.

secrets.descriptionHTML:
Deletes a secret in a repository using the secret name.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.
